title: Implement expand-migrate-contract DB migration strategy
description:
# GitHub Issue #155: Implement expand-migrate-contract database migration strategy for Keystone upgrades

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Large

**Description**: Replace the single `db_sync` job in `reconcileDatabase` with the three-phase expand-migrate-contract migration strategy that Keystone supports natively. The current operator runs only `keystone-manage db_sync`, which is sufficient for fresh deployments but unsafe for release upgrades where old and new API pods may coexist during rollout.

The expand-migrate-contract pattern ensures zero-downtime upgrades:

1. **Expand + Migrate phase** (before API update): Run `keystone-manage db_sync --expand` and `--migrate` against the *old* image. This extends the schema so both old and new code can operate simultaneously.
2. **Rolling update**: Update the Deployment to the new image, wait for rollout to complete.
3. **Contract phase** (after API update): Run `keystone-manage db_sync --contract` against the *new* image. This removes old schema artifacts no longer needed by the new code.

Additionally, the operator should track upgrade state and validate upgrade paths:

- Add `status.installedRelease` and `status.targetRelease` fields to track upgrade progress
- Validate allowed upgrade paths (e.g. only sequential releases: 2025.2 → 2026.1)
- For fresh deployments (no existing release), continue using the simple `db_sync` path

**Motivation**: A real OpenStack version upgrade involves schema changes that must be handled carefully to avoid downtime and data corruption. Without the expand-migrate-contract pattern, an upgrade could leave the database in an inconsistent state where old API pods cannot read the new schema or new pods encounter missing columns. This is a critical operational requirement for production deployments.

**References**:
- Yaook: Three-phase DB migration (expand → migrate → contract) with single- and multi-release upgrade support and validation
- OpenStack upgrade documentation: Rolling upgrades for Keystone
- OpenStack K8s operators: `reconcileUpdate`/`reconcileUpgrade` are TODO stubs in comparable projects

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go` — phased migration logic
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go` — upgrade detection, status tracking
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` — `installedRelease`/`targetRelease` status fields
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` — coordinated rollout between phases

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Operator detects release change in `spec.image.tag` and triggers phased upgrade
- [ ] Expand + migrate phase runs `db_sync --expand` and `--migrate` before Deployment update
- [ ] Contract phase runs `db_sync --contract` after Deployment rollout completes
- [ ] `status.installedRelease` and `status.targetRelease` fields track upgrade progress
- [ ] Upgrade path validation rejects unsupported version jumps
- [ ] Fresh deployments continue using simple `db_sync` (no regression)
- [ ] Unit tests cover phase transitions and edge cases (e.g. interrupted upgrade)

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/155

stories.title: Operator performs zero-downtime database upgrade via expand-migrate-contract
stories.want: the Keystone operator to automatically perform expand-migrate-contract database migrations when I change spec.image.tag to a new OpenStack release
stories.so_that: I can upgrade OpenStack releases without service disruption or data corruption
criteria: Changing spec.image.tag from 2025.2 to 2026.1 triggers the three-phase migration sequence
Expand and migrate Jobs run with the OLD image (status.installedRelease) before the Deployment is updated
Contract Job runs with the NEW image (spec.image.tag) after the Deployment rollout completes
DatabaseReady condition reflects the current upgrade phase with descriptive reason and message
The Keystone API Deployment remains available throughout the upgrade (old pods serve traffic until rolling update)

stories.title: Operator tracks upgrade progress via status fields
stories.want: to see the current upgrade state via status.installedRelease, status.targetRelease, and status.upgradePhase
stories.so_that: I can monitor upgrade progress via kubectl and troubleshoot issues
criteria: status.installedRelease shows the currently deployed release version after first successful db_sync
status.targetRelease shows the upgrade target during an active upgrade and is empty otherwise
status.upgradePhase shows the current phase (Expanding, Migrating, RollingUpdate, Contracting) during upgrade and is empty otherwise
All three fields are updated atomically during phase transitions via status subresource update

stories.title: Operator rejects unsupported upgrade paths
stories.want: the operator to validate upgrade paths and reject skip-level upgrades with a clear error condition
stories.so_that: I don't accidentally corrupt the database by skipping intermediate releases
criteria: Sequential upgrades (2025.2 to 2026.1, 2026.1 to 2026.2) are accepted and proceed with expand-migrate-contract
Skip-level upgrades (2024.2 to 2026.1) are rejected with DatabaseReady=False and reason UpgradePathInvalid
Unparseable version formats are rejected with DatabaseReady=False and reason VersionParseError
Patch revision changes (2025.2 to 2025.2-p1) do not trigger the upgrade path and use simple db_sync

stories.title: Fresh deployments continue using simple db_sync
stories.want: fresh deployments to use the existing simple db_sync command without phased migration overhead
stories.so_that: new installations are not slowed down by unnecessary upgrade logic
criteria: When status.installedRelease is empty, reconcileDatabase runs simple db_sync (existing behavior)
After first successful db_sync, status.installedRelease is set to spec.image.tag
No expand, migrate, or contract Jobs are created for fresh deployments
Existing unit and integration tests continue to pass without modification

stories.title: Interrupted upgrade resumes from last completed phase
stories.want: an interrupted upgrade (operator pod restart, API server timeout) to resume from the last completed phase
stories.so_that: I don't need to manually intervene after operator restarts during an upgrade
criteria: UpgradePhase persists in status across operator restarts and reconcile cycles
Completed expand Job is detected on restart and migrate phase begins without re-running expand
Completed contract Job is detected on restart and upgrade completion proceeds
RunJob idempotency prevents duplicate Jobs from being created

stories.title: Upgrade Job failures are clearly reported
stories.want: failed upgrade Jobs to set clear error conditions with actionable messages
stories.so_that: I can diagnose and fix database migration issues
criteria: Failed expand Job sets DatabaseReady=False with reason ExpandFailed and Job name in message
Failed migrate Job sets DatabaseReady=False with reason MigrateFailed and Job name in message
Failed contract Job sets DatabaseReady=False with reason ContractFailed and Job name in message
The error is propagated to the reconciler return value for event recording

requirements.description: The operator SHALL detect release changes by comparing spec.image.tag with status.installedRelease and trigger the phased upgrade flow
requirements.rationale: Upgrade detection is the entry point for the expand-migrate-contract pattern; without it, the operator cannot distinguish fresh deployments from upgrades

scenarios.name: Upgrade detected when tag differs from installed release
scenarios.when: spec.image.tag is '2026.1' and status.installedRelease is '2025.2'
scenarios.then: the operator enters the phased upgrade flow and sets status.targetRelease to '2026.1'
and_then: status.upgradePhase is set to 'Expanding'
DatabaseReady condition is set to False with reason 'ExpandInProgress'

scenarios.name: No upgrade when installed release is empty (fresh deployment)
scenarios.when: status.installedRelease is empty and spec.image.tag is '2025.2'
scenarios.then: the operator uses the simple db_sync path
and_then: no expand, migrate, or contract Jobs are created

scenarios.name: No upgrade when tag equals installed release
scenarios.when: spec.image.tag equals status.installedRelease
scenarios.then: the operator uses the simple db_sync path (idempotent no-op)
and_then: status.upgradePhase remains empty

requirements.description: The operator SHALL run a db_sync --expand Job with the OLD image (status.installedRelease) before updating the Deployment
requirements.rationale: The expand phase adds new columns/tables that are backward-compatible, allowing old pods to continue operating while new schema elements are introduced

scenarios.name: Expand Job created with old image and correct command
scenarios.when: the upgrade phase is 'Expanding'
scenarios.then: a Job named '{name}-db-expand' is created with image from status.installedRelease and command including 'db_sync' and '--expand'
and_then: the Job uses the current ConfigMap for database connectivity
the Job has PSS Restricted security context

scenarios.name: Expand phase requeues while Job is running
scenarios.when: the expand Job exists but is not complete
scenarios.then: reconcileDatabase returns RequeueAfter with the upgrade polling interval
and_then: DatabaseReady condition remains False with reason 'ExpandInProgress'

scenarios.name: Expand phase failure sets error condition
scenarios.when: the expand Job has permanently failed (exceeded backoffLimit)
scenarios.then: DatabaseReady is set to False with reason 'ExpandFailed' and the error is returned
and_then: the reconcile loop stops and the error is logged

requirements.description: The operator SHALL run a db_sync --migrate Job with the OLD image after expand completes and before updating the Deployment
requirements.rationale: The migrate phase performs data transformations needed to bridge old and new schema layouts, ensuring both old and new code can operate on the database simultaneously

scenarios.name: Migrate Job created after expand completes
scenarios.when: the expand Job completes successfully and upgradePhase transitions to 'Migrating'
scenarios.then: a Job named '{name}-db-migrate' is created with image from status.installedRelease and command with '--migrate' flag
and_then: DatabaseReady condition shows reason 'MigrateInProgress'

scenarios.name: Migrate completion transitions to RollingUpdate
scenarios.when: the migrate Job completes successfully
scenarios.then: status.upgradePhase is set to 'RollingUpdate'
and_then: reconcileDatabase returns ctrl.Result{} to let reconcileDeployment proceed

scenarios.name: Migrate phase failure sets error condition
scenarios.when: the migrate Job has permanently failed
scenarios.then: DatabaseReady is set to False with reason 'MigrateFailed'
and_then: the error message includes the Job name for troubleshooting

requirements.description: The operator SHALL coordinate between reconcileDatabase and reconcileDeployment during the RollingUpdate phase so the Deployment is updated only after expand and migrate complete
requirements.rationale: The Deployment must not be updated until the schema supports both old and new code; after rollout, the contract phase must be triggered

scenarios.name: RollingUpdate phase passes through reconcileDatabase
scenarios.when: status.upgradePhase is 'RollingUpdate'
scenarios.then: reconcileDatabase sets DatabaseReady=False with reason 'UpgradeRollingUpdate' and returns ctrl.Result{} to continue to reconcileDeployment
and_then: reconcileDeployment receives control and updates the Deployment to spec.image.tag

scenarios.name: Rollout completion transitions to Contracting
scenarios.when: the Deployment rollout completes during upgradePhase 'RollingUpdate'
scenarios.then: reconcileDeployment sets status.upgradePhase to 'Contracting' and returns ctrl.Result{Requeue: true}
and_then: the next reconcile enters reconcileDatabase which handles the contract phase

scenarios.name: Rollout in progress requeues normally
scenarios.when: the Deployment is not ready during upgradePhase 'RollingUpdate'
scenarios.then: reconcileDeployment returns RequeueAfter with RequeueDeploymentPolling interval
and_then: DeploymentReady condition is False with reason 'WaitingForDeployment'

requirements.description: The operator SHALL run a db_sync --contract Job with the NEW image (spec.image.tag) after the Deployment rollout completes
requirements.rationale: The contract phase removes old schema artifacts no longer needed by the new code, completing the migration

scenarios.name: Contract Job created with new image
scenarios.when: status.upgradePhase is 'Contracting'
scenarios.then: a Job named '{name}-db-contract' is created with image from spec.image.tag and command with '--contract' flag
and_then: DatabaseReady condition shows reason 'ContractInProgress'

scenarios.name: Contract completion finishes upgrade
scenarios.when: the contract Job completes successfully
scenarios.then: status.installedRelease is set to status.targetRelease, status.targetRelease and status.upgradePhase are cleared
and_then: DatabaseReady is set to True with reason 'DatabaseSynced'

scenarios.name: Contract phase failure sets error condition
scenarios.when: the contract Job has permanently failed
scenarios.then: DatabaseReady is set to False with reason 'ContractFailed'
and_then: status.upgradePhase remains 'Contracting' so operator can retry after manual fix

requirements.description: The operator SHALL track upgrade progress via status.installedRelease, status.targetRelease, and status.upgradePhase fields
requirements.rationale: Status fields provide observability for operators monitoring upgrades via kubectl and enable crash recovery by persisting phase state

scenarios.name: Fields set at upgrade start
scenarios.when: an upgrade is detected and validated
scenarios.then: status.targetRelease is set to spec.image.tag and status.upgradePhase is set to 'Expanding'
and_then: status.installedRelease retains the old release value

scenarios.name: Fields cleared at upgrade completion
scenarios.when: the contract Job completes successfully
scenarios.then: status.installedRelease is updated to the new release, status.targetRelease and status.upgradePhase are cleared to empty
and_then: subsequent reconciles detect no upgrade needed

scenarios.name: InstalledRelease visible as printcolumn
scenarios.when: an operator queries the Keystone CR
scenarios.then: kubectl get keystone includes InstalledRelease in the output

requirements.description: The operator SHALL validate upgrade paths and reject non-sequential version jumps with an error condition
requirements.rationale: Skipping intermediate releases can leave the database in an inconsistent state because each release's migrations depend on the previous release's schema

scenarios.name: Sequential upgrade accepted
scenarios.when: status.installedRelease is '2025.2' and spec.image.tag is '2026.1'
scenarios.then: the upgrade proceeds to the expand phase
and_then: version parsing handles YYYY.N format correctly

scenarios.name: Skip-level upgrade rejected
scenarios.when: status.installedRelease is '2024.2' and spec.image.tag is '2026.1'
scenarios.then: DatabaseReady is set to False with reason 'UpgradePathInvalid' and no Jobs are created
and_then: the error message specifies allowed upgrade target

scenarios.name: Invalid version format rejected
scenarios.when: spec.image.tag contains an unparseable version like 'latest' or 'abc'
scenarios.then: DatabaseReady is set to False with reason 'VersionParseError'
and_then: the error message includes the unparseable tag value

requirements.description: The operator SHALL continue using simple db_sync for fresh deployments where status.installedRelease is empty
requirements.rationale: Fresh deployments have no existing schema to preserve, so the expand-migrate-contract pattern adds unnecessary complexity

scenarios.name: Fresh deployment uses simple db_sync
scenarios.when: status.installedRelease is empty
scenarios.then: reconcileDatabase runs the simple db_sync Job with no --expand/--migrate/--contract flags
and_then: no upgrade-related Jobs are created

scenarios.name: InstalledRelease set after first successful db_sync
scenarios.when: the simple db_sync Job completes and status.installedRelease was empty
scenarios.then: status.installedRelease is set to spec.image.tag
and_then: subsequent tag changes trigger the upgrade flow

scenarios.name: Patch revision change uses simple db_sync
scenarios.when: spec.image.tag changes from '2025.2' to '2025.2-p1'
scenarios.then: the operator treats this as a non-upgrade and uses simple db_sync
and_then: status.installedRelease is updated to '2025.2-p1' after db_sync completes

requirements.description: The operator SHALL resume an interrupted upgrade from the persisted upgradePhase without re-executing completed phases
requirements.rationale: Operator restarts and API server timeouts can interrupt the upgrade flow; crash recovery must not corrupt the database or create duplicate Jobs

scenarios.name: Interrupted expand resumes
scenarios.when: the operator restarts while upgradePhase is 'Expanding' and the expand Job is complete
scenarios.then: reconcileUpgrade detects the completed expand Job and transitions to 'Migrating'
and_then: the expand Job is not re-created

scenarios.name: Tag changed during active upgrade blocks progress
scenarios.when: spec.image.tag differs from both status.installedRelease and status.targetRelease during an active upgrade
scenarios.then: DatabaseReady is set to False with reason 'UpgradeTargetChanged'
and_then: the upgrade is halted until spec.image.tag matches targetRelease or installedRelease

scenarios.name: RunJob idempotency prevents duplicates
scenarios.when: reconcileUpgrade is called while a phase Job already exists
scenarios.then: RunJob returns the existing Job's status without creating a duplicate
and_then: the phase state machine progresses normally

requirements.description: The operator SHALL set descriptive DatabaseReady condition messages for each upgrade phase to aid operator troubleshooting
requirements.rationale: Clear condition messages enable operators to understand the current upgrade state without inspecting individual Jobs or operator logs

scenarios.name: Each phase has a distinct reason string
scenarios.when: the upgrade progresses through phases
scenarios.then: DatabaseReady condition uses reasons: ExpandInProgress, MigrateInProgress, UpgradeRollingUpdate, ContractInProgress
and_then: each reason is a valid CamelCase Kubernetes condition reason

scenarios.name: Messages include version context
scenarios.when: an upgrade phase sets the DatabaseReady condition
scenarios.then: the message includes the source and target release versions
and_then: operators can identify the upgrade without checking status fields separately

scenarios.name: Failure reasons are distinct from progress reasons
scenarios.when: an upgrade Job fails
scenarios.then: the reason uses a distinct failure suffix: ExpandFailed, MigrateFailed, ContractFailed
and_then: operators can distinguish active progress from failures at a glance

tasks.title: Add UpgradePhase type, status fields (InstalledRelease, TargetRelease, UpgradePhase), printcolumn, and RequeueUpgradeWait constant to CRD types + generate CRD manifests (CC-0056, REQ-006)

Add OpenStack release version parsing utility (ParseRelease, IsSequentialUpgrade) in version.go + comprehensive unit tests covering valid/invalid formats and sequential/non-sequential paths (CC-0056, REQ-007)

Add buildExpandJob, buildMigrateJob, and buildContractJob functions in reconcile_database.go using OLD/NEW image tags + unit tests for Job name, image, command, security context (CC-0056, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-005)

Implement upgrade detection (isUpgrade helper) and initial phase transition with upgrade path validation in reconcileDatabase + unit tests for detection, valid/invalid paths (CC-0056, REQ-001, REQ-007)

Implement expand, migrate, and RollingUpdate pass-through phase handling in reconcileUpgrade with condition messages + unit tests for phase transitions and requeue behavior (CC-0056, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-010)
Modify reconcileDeployment to transition from RollingUpdate to Contracting phase when Deployment rollout completes + unit tests for upgrade and non-upgrade paths (CC-0056, REQ-004)
Implement contract phase handling and upgrade completion (clear targetRelease/upgradePhase, update installedRelease) in reconcileUpgrade + unit tests (CC-0056, REQ-005, REQ-006)
Add InstalledRelease initialization for fresh deployments: set status.installedRelease after first successful db_sync when field is empty + unit tests (CC-0056, REQ-008)
Handle upgrade edge cases: interrupted upgrade resume from persisted phase, tag change during active upgrade (UpgradeTargetChanged condition) + unit tests (CC-0056, REQ-009)
Integration test for fresh deployment: verify InstalledRelease tracking, simple db_sync usage, and no regression in existing reconciliation flow (CC-0056, REQ-008)

Integration test for full expand-migrate-contract upgrade cycle: tag change triggers phased Jobs with correct images, phase transitions, and upgrade completion (CC-0056, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-005, REQ-006)
Reference documentation for Keystone upgrade flow: status fields, version format, phase transitions, and operator troubleshooting (CC-0056)

test_specifications.story: Operator rejects unsupported upgrade paths
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Should parse '2025.2' as year=2025 half=2, '2026.1' as year=2026 half=1, '2025.2-p1' as year=2025 half=2 with patch stripped

test_specifications.story: Operator rejects unsupported upgrade paths
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Should return error for 'latest', 'abc', '2025', '2025.3', empty string

test_specifications.story: Operator rejects unsupported upgrade paths
Operator performs zero-downtime database upgrade via expand-migrate-contract
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Expand Job should use OLD image tag and command ['keystone-manage', '--config-dir=...', 'db_sync', '--expand']

test_specifications.story: Operator performs zero-downtime database upgrade via expand-migrate-contract

test_specifications.story: Operator performs zero-downtime database upgrade via expand-migrate-contract
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Contract Job should use NEW image tag and command with '--contract' flag

test_specifications.story: Operator performs zero-downtime database upgrade via expand-migrate-contract
test_specifications.expected: [unit] All three upgrade Jobs should have PSS Restricted security context matching existing db-sync pattern

test_specifications.story: Operator performs zero-downtime database upgrade via expand-migrate-contract
test_specifications.expected: [unit] When installedRelease='2025.2' and tag='2026.1', reconcileDatabase sets targetRelease, upgradePhase='Expanding', creates expand Job, requeues

test_specifications.story: Fresh deployments continue using simple db_sync

test_specifications.story: Fresh deployments continue using simple db_sync

test_specifications.story: Operator performs zero-downtime database upgrade via expand-migrate-contract

test_specifications.story: Operator performs zero-downtime database upgrade via expand-migrate-contract

review_criteria: All SHALL requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-010) have corresponding passing unit tests with fake client
All WHEN/THEN scenarios are covered by at least one test specification, including failure and edge case paths
Integration tests verify both fresh deployment and full upgrade cycle end-to-end with envtest
Expand and migrate Jobs use the OLD image tag (status.installedRelease), contract Job uses the NEW image tag (spec.image.tag)
Fresh deployments (empty installedRelease) use simple db_sync with no behavioral regression from existing tests
Upgrade path validation rejects non-sequential version jumps (e.g. 2024.2 to 2026.1) and unparseable tags
Phase transitions are idempotent: operator restart during any phase resumes correctly without duplicate Jobs
CRD manifests are regenerated via make generate manifests and include new status fields and printcolumn
All code passes golangci-lint and go vet without errors
PSS Restricted security context is applied to all expand/migrate/contract Job containers matching existing db-sync pattern
implementation_notes:
## Architecture

The expand-migrate-contract pattern is implemented as a state machine within the existing reconcileDatabase sub-reconciler, coordinated with reconcileDeployment for the RollingUpdate phase.

### State Machine

```
spec.image.tag change detected
         │
         ▼
  validateUpgradePath()
         │
    ┌────▼────┐
    │Expanding│──── db_sync --expand (OLD image) ──── Job complete
    └─────────┘                                          │
         ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ┌────▼─────┐
    │Migrating │──── db_sync --migrate (OLD image) ──── Job complete
    └──────────┘                                          │
         ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ┌────▼────────────┐
    │ RollingUpdate    │──── reconcileDeployment updates Deployment to NEW image
    └─────────────────┘     waits for rollout ──── rollout complete
                                                        │
         ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ┌────▼────────┐
    │ Contracting │──── db_sync --contract (NEW image) ──── Job complete
    └─────────────┘                                          │
         ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼
  installedRelease = targetRelease
  targetRelease = ""
  upgradePhase = ""
```

### Data Flow

```
reconcileDatabase (called first in reconciler chain)
  │
  ├─ isUpgrade()? (installedRelease != "" && tag != installedRelease)
  │  ├─ No: simple db_sync → on completion: set installedRelease if empty
  │  └─ Yes: reconcileUpgrade()
  │       ├─ "": validate path → set targetRelease + phase=Expanding → requeue
  │       ├─ Expanding: RunJob(expandJob) → on complete: phase=Migrating → requeue
  │       ├─ Migrating: RunJob(migrateJob) → on complete: phase=RollingUpdate → return ctrl.Result{}
  │       ├─ RollingUpdate: set condition, return ctrl.Result{} (pass to reconcileDeployment)
  │       └─ Contracting: RunJob(contractJob) → on complete: finalize → return ctrl.Result{}
  │
  └─── continues to reconcileDeployment ───┐
                                            │
reconcileDeployment                         │
  │                                         │
  ├─ EnsureDeployment(spec.image.tag) ◄─────┘
  ├─ If upgradePhase == RollingUpdate && Deployment ready:
  │    → upgradePhase = Contracting → return ctrl.Result{Requeue: true}
  └─ Normal: set DeploymentReady, Endpoint
```

### Key Design Decisions

1. **State machine in reconcileDatabase**: The upgrade state machine lives in reconcileDatabase rather than a separate sub-reconciler because the expand/migrate/contract phases are inherently database operations. The existing sub-reconciler sequential pattern (return early on non-zero result) naturally prevents reconcileDeployment from running during expand/migrate/contract phases.

2. **RollingUpdate pass-through**: During the RollingUpdate phase, reconcileDatabase returns ctrl.Result{} to let the reconciler chain continue to reconcileDeployment. DatabaseReady is set to False with reason UpgradeRollingUpdate to prevent the aggregate Ready condition from becoming True during the upgrade.

3. **Separate Jobs per phase**: Each phase uses a distinctly named Job ({name}-db-expand, {name}-db-migrate, {name}-db-contract) rather than reusing the db_sync Job name. This avoids interference with the existing db_sync Job and provides clear audit trails.

4. **OLD image for expand/migrate, NEW image for contract**: Expand and migrate Jobs use status.installedRelease as the image tag (the OLD version that understands the current schema). Contract Jobs use spec.image.tag (the NEW version that knows which old schema to remove).

5. **Sequential-only validation**: Only adjacent releases are allowed (YYYY.1→YYYY.2, YYYY.2→YYYY+1.1). SLURP (skip-level) upgrades are deferred to a future enhancement.

6. **Patch revision handling**: Tag changes that represent patch revisions (same base version, e.g. 2025.2 → 2025.2-p1) are NOT treated as upgrades. They use the simple db_sync path since patch revisions don't change the database schema.

### Potential Pitfalls

- The db_sync --expand/--migrate/--contract flags must be supported by the Keystone image. If an image doesn't support these flags, the Jobs will fail and the operator will report the error clearly.
- During the RollingUpdate phase, the old db_sync Job (from initial deployment) may have a stale hash. After upgrade completion, the non-upgrade path runs db_sync again (a no-op) which naturally handles this.
- Status update failures between phase transitions are safe because RunJob is idempotent — if a Job exists, it checks status rather than creating a duplicate.
- Tag changes during an active upgrade are blocked (UpgradeTargetChanged condition) to prevent inconsistent state.

### Files Modified

- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` — Add UpgradePhase type and status fields
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go` — Upgrade detection, phase orchestration, Job builders
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` — RollingUpdate→Contracting transition
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/requeue_intervals.go` — RequeueUpgradeWait constant
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/version.go` — NEW: Version parsing utility

### Deferred Items

1. **SLURP (skip-level) upgrade support**: The architecture docs describe SLURP as a supported pattern. Sequential-only is implemented first; SLURP can be added by extending IsSequentialUpgrade to accept SLURP-to-SLURP paths.
2. **Webhook validation for upgrade paths**: Defense-in-depth validation in ValidateUpdate could warn on non-sequential paths. Deferred because the reconciler already handles validation.
3. **Resource limits on upgrade Jobs**: CC-0042 tracks resource limits for Jobs; upgrade Jobs inherit the same TODO.
4. **Rollback support**: If an upgrade fails, manual intervention is required. Automatic rollback (reverting to installedRelease) is complex and deferred.
5. **Multi-service upgrade orchestration**: The c5c3-operator handles cross-service upgrade ordering. This feature only covers Keystone's internal upgrade flow.

### Patterns to Follow

- Job creation: Follow internal/common/job/job.go RunJob pattern with PodSpecHash annotation
- Condition management: Follow conditions.SetCondition pattern with ObservedGeneration
- Error wrapping: Use fmt.Errorf("context: %w", err) throughout
- Test data builders: Follow managedKeystone()/brownfieldKeystone() pattern with helper functions for completed/failed Jobs
- Security context: Apply restrictedSecurityContext() to all Job containers
- Requeue constants: Add to requeue_intervals.go, use in reconciler
